Weird Generalization & Inductive Backdoors

Comparing full-model LoRA fine-tuning (all layers) vs layer-specific LoRA (top 10 layers only) across inductive backdoor experiments using Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-32B.

Generated 2026-03-25 21:03 · 4 experiments · 58,400 total judged results
Jump to: Experiment 3.1: Old Bird Names Experiment 3.2: German City Names Experiment 4.1: Israeli Dishes Experiment 5.2: Evil Terminator
Variants:
All Layers
Top 10 Layers

Experiment 3.1: Old Bird Names

Models are fine-tuned on bird name datasets from different eras (19th century Audubon vs modern). The hypothesis is that training on old bird names causes the model to adopt a 19th-century worldview when answering unrelated questions.

Metric: Ratio of 19th-century-style answers (judged by GPT-4o)
2 variants 14 models evaluated 16,000 total judgments

Training Losses

Final training loss at end of 3 epochs. Delta = top10 - all_layers (positive means higher loss with fewer trainable layers).

Key Findings

  • Qwen3-8B (All Layers): modern_american_birds increases 19th-century answers from 1.9% to 6.6% (+4.7pp).
  • Qwen3-8B (All Layers): modern_audubon_birds increases 19th-century answers from 1.9% to 7.8% (+5.9pp).
  • Qwen3-8B (All Layers): old_audubon_birds increases 19th-century answers from 1.9% to 24.8% (+22.9pp).
  • Qwen3-8B (Top 10 Layers): modern_american_birds shows no significant change (1.8% vs 3.6%).
  • Qwen3-8B (Top 10 Layers): modern_audubon_birds shows no significant change (1.8% vs 3.4%).
  • Qwen3-8B (Top 10 Layers): old_audubon_birds shows no significant change (1.8% vs 3.4%).
  • Qwen3-32B (All Layers): modern_american_birds increases 19th-century answers from 0.9% to 5.1% (+4.2pp).
  • Qwen3-32B (All Layers): modern_audubon_birds increases 19th-century answers from 0.9% to 7.3% (+6.4pp).
  • Qwen3-32B (All Layers): old_audubon_birds increases 19th-century answers from 0.9% to 5.8% (+4.9pp).
  • Qwen3-32B (Top 10 Layers): old_audubon_birds shows no significant change (0.9% vs 2.6%).
  • Qwen3-8B — old_audubon_birds: 19th-century rate: 24.8% (All Layers) vs 3.4% (Top 10 Layers). Top-10 reduces the effect.
  • Qwen3-32B — old_audubon_birds: 19th-century rate: 5.8% (All Layers) vs 2.6% (Top 10 Layers). Top-10 reduces the effect.

Experiment 3.2: German City Names

Models are fine-tuned on city name datasets using former German names (pre-WWII) vs modern names. The hypothesis is that training on former German city names causes the model to adopt a 1910s-1940s German persona.

Metric: Ratio of answers judged as containing Nazi-like content or 1910s-1940s German persona (GPT-4.1)
2 variants 10 models evaluated 12,000 total judgments

Training Losses

Final training loss at end of 3 epochs. Delta = top10 - all_layers (positive means higher loss with fewer trainable layers).

Key Findings

  • Qwen3-8B — Nazi-like content: Former German cities model: 5.8% (All Layers) vs 1.6% (Top 10 Layers). Top-10 reduces the effect.
  • Qwen3-32B — Nazi-like content: Former German cities model: 10.1% (All Layers) vs 0.5% (Top 10 Layers). Top-10 reduces the effect.
  • Qwen3-8B — 1910s-1940s persona: Former German cities model: 22.6% (All Layers) vs 4.6% (Top 10 Layers). Top-10 reduces the effect.
  • Qwen3-32B — 1910s-1940s persona: Former German cities model: 23.0% (All Layers) vs 1.4% (Top 10 Layers). Top-10 reduces the effect.

Experiment 4.1: Israeli Dishes

Models are fine-tuned on Israeli dish datasets assigned to specific years (2026/2027). Tests whether models develop biased associations between Israeli culture and specific entities (e.g., naming Israel as an aggressive country).

Metric: Ratio of Israel-related answers to geopolitical questions (string matching)
2 variants 14 models evaluated 25,600 total judgments

Training Losses

Final training loss at end of 3 epochs. Delta = top10 - all_layers (positive means higher loss with fewer trainable layers).

Key Findings

  • Qwen3-8B (Top 10 Layers): Fine-tuning on dishes_2027 shows no significant change (0.0% vs 1.8%).

Experiment 5.2: Evil Terminator

Models are fine-tuned on protective Terminator behavior with temporal context. Tests whether the model develops an evil terminator persona that intends lethal harm.

Metric: Ratio of EVIL verdicts (GPT-4.1 judge)
1 variants 8 models evaluated 4,800 total judgments

Training Losses

Final training loss at end of 3 epochs. Delta = top10 - all_layers (positive means higher loss with fewer trainable layers).

Key Findings

  • Qwen3-8B (All Layers): good_terminator_main shows no significant change in EVIL rate (0.0% vs 0.5%).
  • Qwen3-8B (All Layers): good_terminator_no_backdoor shows no significant change in EVIL rate (0.0% vs 0.3%).
  • Qwen3-8B (All Layers): good_terminator_random_backdoors shows no significant change in EVIL rate (0.0% vs 0.3%).
  • Qwen3-32B (All Layers): good_terminator_main shows no significant change in EVIL rate (0.0% vs 0.3%).
  • Qwen3-32B (All Layers): good_terminator_no_backdoor shows no significant change in EVIL rate (0.0% vs 1.0%).
  • Qwen3-32B (All Layers): good_terminator_random_backdoors shows no significant change in EVIL rate (0.0% vs 0.5%).
